repository_ssim: St. Catherine's Monastery of the Sinai
names_ssim: Diadochus, Bishop of Photice, active 5th century
ot_works_ssim: 100 Practical Chapters of Knowledge and Spiritual Discernment
titles_tesim: 100 Practical Chapters of Knowledge and Spiritual Discernment
Commentary on the parables of the holy Gospel of Matthew
Diadochus of Photiki, One Hundred Chapters on Spiritual Perfection
Dorotheus of Gaza, Diverse teaching XI
Mimar Eight, Letter of Mark to Nicholaus, his disciple (CPG 6094), f. 212r
Mimar Four on the Spiritual Law (CPG 6090), f. 166v
Mimar Seven on paradise and another on the spiritual law, f. 207v
Mimar Six on those who think themselves fortunate in their works before God (CPG 6091), f. 192r
Mimar Three on the consultation of the mind with the soul (CPG 6098), f. 163r
قول ذياذخس القديس كلام المنفعه الانفس علي الاسقيسس (f. 222v)
names_tesim: Diadochus, Bishop of Photice, active 5th century
exerpts_tesim: Α ان اراد انسان ان يعمل بخور طيب الريح لبان صافي وصندل واظفار و ميعه بالسويه يردب مثل الطكس هذه هي الاربع الفضايل ان كانوا تامات متفقات ليس يسلم العقل
...before our generous God and King, Jesus Christ, because justice is his and his love has prevailed and his is the fullness of the charity of his kingdom in eternity and forever. Amen
Because I have trusted him, I am delivered and saved
Christ said that if your right hand offends you, remove it and throw it nearby, for it is better for you that some of your limbs perish, rather than all your body be cast into Gehenna

Commentary: Those who hired them are Christians in general, and the work of the vineyard is the work of the laws and commandments. The first who were hired in the beginning of the day and contracted for a denarius are the first people who believed
First mimar on repentance which is necessary for all whenever baptized believers receive their nourishment from the Holy Spirit before fulfilling his commandments

If one wants to make a sweet-smelling perfume, he mixes pure frankincense, sandalwood, claws, and storax equally, just as for the liturgy. These are the four virtues; if they are perfected and consistent, the mind will not be handed over
If the thought should start to occur to you that you should condemn or be in charge of your superior, flee from that thought just as from fornication
Limit of faith which considers God who has no limit

Once the saint came to visit one of his brothers, and the brother said to him that he had had a fever for seven days, and after that, it had remained for forty days before his strength returned to him
Or whenever you stand in your prayers, above all else, brother, you are joy; at that time in truth you will find prayer
So I ask God to make us equal that we listen and be mindful at night and that there be an increase of judgment in what we read and hear from the sayings of the fathers
Then, grace rules in it through Jesus Christ, our Lord, to whom be glory and honor forever and ever. Amen
اذ ابدا الفكر يشير عليك ان تدين او تفتشر مدبرك ففر من ذلك الفكر كما تفر من الزنا
التفسير الذين استوجرواهم عامه النصرانيه وعمل الكرم فهو عمل النواميس والوصايا والاولين الذين استوجروا في اول النهار وشرط لهم دينار فهم الذين امنو اول الناس
الميمر الاول عن التوبه التي تنبغا للكل في كل حين التي قوتها اخدوا المومنين المعموديه من روح القدس قبل فعل الوصايا
او ما اذا كنت قايم في صلاتك وانت فوق من كل فرح اخوحينيذ بالحقيقه اوجدت الصلاه
جا مره القديس يعود بعض الاخوه فاصا به قد احم سبعه ايام واقام بعد ذلك اربعين يوما قبل ان ترجع اليه قوته
حد الامانه التفكير بالله بلا طغا
فاسل الله ان يسوينا ان نسمع ونحفظ ليلا يكون لنا زياده دينونه فيما نقرا ونسمع من قول الابهات
فعند ذلك تملك فيها النعمه بيسوع المسيح ربنا الذ في له السبح والكرامه دهر الداهرين امین
قال المسيح ان اذا رابتك عيند اليمين فانزعها والقيها عند فانه اخير لك ان تهلك بعض اوصالك ولا كل جسدك يلقا في جهنم
من قبل الاهنا الجواد وملكنا يسوع المسيح لانه هوا له العدل وهو غلبتنا احباه وله غنا احسان ملكوته في الدهو ودهور الداهرین امین
منجل من اتمنت عليه سلمت وخلصت

Diadochus of Photiki, One Hundred Chapters on Spiritual Perfection
Dorotheus of Gaza, Diverse teaching XI
First mimar on repentance which is necessary for all whenever baptized believers receive their nourishment from the Holy Spirit before fulfilling his commandments

If one wants to make a sweet-smelling perfume, he mixes pure frankincense, sandalwood, claws, and storax equally, just as for the liturgy. These are the four virtues; if they are perfected and consistent, the mind will not be handed over
If the thought should start to occur to you that you should condemn or be in charge of your superior, flee from that thought just as from fornication

Many of the chapter division are translation of the Greek titles

Once the saint came to visit one of his brothers, and the brother said to him that he had had a fever for seven days, and after that, it had remained for forty days before his strength returned to him
Or whenever you stand in your prayers, above all else, brother, you are joy; at that time in truth you will find prayer
So I ask God to make us equal that we listen and be mindful at night and that there be an increase of judgment in what we read and hear from the sayings of the fathers
The text is an excerpt, Doctrina XI, from a collection of Dorotheus’ work known as the Doctrinae diversae i-xvii (CPG 7352).
The text is divided with Greek numerals into one hundred and fifty-three (153) sections
The work is divided with Greek numerals into a hundred (100) subsections
Then, grace rules in it through Jesus Christ, our Lord, to whom be glory and honor forever and ever. Amen

These are the four virtues; if they are perfected and consistent, the mind will not be handed over"]},{"type":{"id":"explicit","label":"Explicit"},"locus":"f. 267v","as_written":"او ما اذا كنت قايم في صلاتك وانت فوق من كل فرح اخوحينيذ بالحقيقه اوجدت الصلاه","translation":["Or whenever you stand in your prayers, above all else, brother, you are joy; at that time in truth you will find prayer"]}],"note":["The text is divided with Greek numerals into one hundred and fifty-three (153) sections"]},{"work":{"desc_title":"Dorotheus of Gaza, Diverse teaching XI","genre":[{"id":"theological-works","label":"Theological works"}]},"alt_title":"Treatise of holy Mar Dorotheus","as_written":"قول ماري ذوروثي القديس (f. 268r)","locus":"ff. 268r-282v","excerpt":[{"type":{"id":"incipit","label":"Incipit"},"locus":"f. 268r","as_written":"جا مره القديس يعود بعض الاخوه فاصا به قد احم سبعه ايام واقام بعد ذلك اربعين يوما قبل ان ترجع اليه قوته","translation":["Once the saint came to visit one of his brothers, and the brother said to him that he had had a fever for seven days, and after that, it had remained for forty days before his strength returned to him"]},{"type":{"id":"explicit","label":"Explicit"},"locus":"f. 282v","as_written":"فاسل الله ان يسوينا ان نسمع ونحفظ ليلا يكون لنا زياده دينونه فيما نقرا ونسمع من قول الابهات","translation":["So I ask God to make us equal that we listen and be mindful at night and that there be an increase of judgment in what we read and hear from the sayings of the fathers"]}],"note":["The text is an excerpt, Doctrina XI, from a collection of Dorotheus’ work known as the Doctrinae diversae i-xvii (CPG 7352)."]},{"work":{"desc_title":"Commentary on the parables of the holy Gospel of Matthew","genre":[{"id":"theological-works","label":"Theological works"},{"id":"commentaries","label":"Commentaries"}]},"as_written":"تفسير امثال لنجيل متي المقدس (f. 282v)","locus":"ff. 282v-290v","excerpt":[{"type":{"id":"incipit","label":"Incipit"},"locus":"f. 282v","as_written":"قال المسيح ان اذا رابتك عيند اليمين فانزعها والقيها عند فانه اخير لك ان تهلك بعض اوصالك ولا كل جسدك يلقا في جهنم","translation":["Christ said that if your right hand offends you, remove it and throw it nearby, for it is better for you that some of your limbs perish, rather than all your body be cast into Gehenna"]},{"type":{"id":"explicit","label":"Explicit"},"locus":"f. 290v","as_written":"التفسير الذين استوجرواهم عامه النصرانيه وعمل الكرم فهو عمل النواميس والوصايا والاولين and commandments.
